steps.title: Close the interrupting phrase
steps.content: The phrase also known as the greater adjutant interrupts the main clause between Leptoptilos dubius and can be found. Because the interruption starts after a comma, it should close with a comma.

steps.title: Use matching commas
steps.content: Choice A uses a semicolon where the sentence is not separating independent clauses. Choice B leaves the interruption unclosed. Choice D shifts to an unmatched dash. The remaining choice closes the interrupting phrase with a comma. The answer is C.
